config.title: Seeing a Football Match in Berlin: FAQ
config.subtitle: Which Berlin club plays in which league, what tickets actually cost, why Union games sell out, and how to get to each ground.

items.q: Which Berlin football club plays in the Bundesliga?
items.a: 1. FC Union Berlin. For the 2026/27 season Union play in the Bundesliga at Stadion An der Alten Försterei in Köpenick, and Hertha BSC play in the 2. Bundesliga at the Olympiastadion. This catches most visitors out, because Hertha is the more familiar name internationally but is currently the second-division club.

items.q: How much does a football ticket in Berlin cost?
items.a: At Hertha BSC a standing ticket costs 17 EUR and seated tickets run from 27 EUR up to about 57 EUR depending on the seat and the opponent. Children up to 13 get 40 percent off, and one adult can bring up to two children in free under the Kids4Free scheme. At Union Berlin standing tickets start at around 15 EUR, but availability is the problem rather than the price.

items.q: Why is it so hard to get Union Berlin tickets?
items.a: The ground holds only 22,012 people, which is very small for a Bundesliga club, and club members get exclusive buying windows before anything reaches a public sale. For the bigger fixtures very little survives to general sale. Union tickets are also personalised and cannot be passed on, and the club states that tickets bought on resale marketplaces lose their validity, so you would be refused entry.

items.q: Can I buy a Berlin football ticket on a resale site?
items.a: Not safely. Union Berlin state that tickets bought through resale platforms are void, which means no entry and no refund. Hertha BSC route official resale through their own Clubsale. If you need a resale ticket, use the club's own exchange and nothing else.

items.q: How do I get to Stadion An der Alten Försterei?
items.a: Take the S3 from Alexanderplatz towards Erkner to S Köpenick, then walk about 15 minutes to the ground at An der Wuhlheide 263. Allow around 40 minutes from central Berlin. Coming from BER airport, take the S45 or S9 to Schöneweide and change to tram 60 or 67, which stops beside the stadium. Gates open 120 minutes before kickoff.

items.q: How do I get to the Olympiastadion for a Hertha match?
items.a: Take the U2 from Alexanderplatz towards Ruhleben to Olympia-Stadion and walk about 5 minutes. The S3 and S9 also stop at Olympiastadion, slightly closer to the east gate. Around 40 minutes from the centre, and gates open 90 minutes before kickoff. Both grounds are inside the normal AB fare area.

items.q: Does my match ticket include public transport in Berlin?
items.a: Do not assume it does. Some German clubs include travel with the match ticket and some do not, and the information for the two Berlin clubs is not consistent. Riding without a valid ticket costs 60 EUR, so check what your specific ticket says. If it does not clearly state that travel is included, buy a normal BVG ticket.

items.q: Can I still see a game if my dates do not match a Bundesliga fixture?
items.a: Often yes. Union Berlin's women's team play in the Frauen-Bundesliga at the same Köpenick ground, with far easier ticket availability, and their season opens at home against Bayern Munich on Friday 21 August 2026 at 18:20. Hertha home games in the 2. Bundesliga are also usually available close to the date.

items.q: Why does my fixture only show a weekend and not a kickoff time?
items.a: Because that is all that has been fixed. The German leagues published the full 2026/27 fixture lists on 2 July 2026, but only the match weekend is set at that point. Exact kickoff times are confirmed a few weeks ahead once television picks its slots, so a game can end up on a Friday night or a Sunday evening. If a match is the reason for your travel dates, check the club site again about four weeks before.

items.q: What can I take into a Berlin stadium?
items.a: Nothing bigger than A4 at either ground. Union do not allow backpacks at all and Hertha allow bags up to A4 with a depth of 15 cm. Both run a left-luggage service, 4 EUR at Union and 5 EUR at Hertha. Hertha do not allow plastic or glass bottles, though tetrapaks up to one litre are fine. Both stadiums take cash and normal cards, so you do not need to load a stadium payment card.
